meaning: adj.阳光照射不到的,阴暗的
examples: Carmen stayed behind in the dark, sunless room.
The day dawned sunless and with a low cloud base.
Suspended on their stilts, the actors remove their masks and reveal their off-white faces, staring terrified at a sunless sky, dark clouds congregating.
The ugly creature hails from the sunless depths of the Pacific off the coast of New Zealand.
Lasers beamed from landing craft could, he says, power rovers in sunless areas of the moon or Mars, such as craters where water might be found.
